2026-04-14T00:06:34.0445143Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0445870Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0446722Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0447051Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0447293Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0447455Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0447639Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0447817Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0448045Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0448391Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0448785Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-14T00:06:34.0449050Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-14T00:15:15.9775361Z

2026-04-14T00:15:17.2000217Z 3. 🟡 **Medium** — Field-level `beta` JSDoc still lists "Evaluation Rules" which is a top-level route, not a beta sub-route
2026-04-14T00:15:17.2124075Z 4. 🟡 **Low** — CHANGELOG breaking change for `container_protocol_versions` doesn't name the affected type(s)

2026-04-14T00:15:21.1865548Z   Looking at the CHANGELOG for v2.1.0, I can see the PR adds three new routes: `project.beta.agents` for managed agent operations, `project.beta.skills` for skill access, and `project.beta.toolboxes` for toolbox features. The BetaOperations interface reflects these additions with `skills` and `toolboxes` properties, though neither has documentation comments yet.
2026-04-14T00:15:21.1867198Z   
2026-04-14T00:15:21.1868630Z   I'm noticing a pattern here—`skills`, `toolboxes`, and `schedules` are all missing JSDoc comments while the other properties like `redTeams`, `memoryStores`, `insights`, `evaluators`, `evaluationTaxonomies`, and `agents` all have them.
